commandsRun.observation: 12 targeted real-runtime Playwright tests passed in 26.5s, covering timer view persistence, cross-workspace running-header continuity, editable/stoppable foreign-workspace running timers, workspace-scoped history, and running-header elapsed-time behavior.

assertions.reason: Playwright test `VAL-ENTRY-001: selected TimerView persists after refresh` passed, proving calendar/list/timesheet remain one /timer page family with persisted TimerView preference.
Cross-workspace running-header Playwright test passed, proving the same running timer remains visible after workspace switch.
Cross-workspace running-header Playwright test passed, proving the foreign-workspace running timer can be edited and stopped from the shared header.
History-scoping Playwright tests passed, proving calendar/list/timesheet remain scoped to the current workspace and exclude foreign-workspace history.
Running-header Playwright tests passed, proving live elapsed time derives from start time and stop clears back to idle state.
Workspace-switch Playwright test passed, proving a global running timer stays visible while history immediately re-scopes to the newly selected workspace.
Cross-workspace stop Playwright test passed, proving stopping a foreign-workspace running timer clears current state without leaking that stopped entry into current-workspace history.
TimerView persistence Playwright test passed, proving the selected timer view survives workspace switch while the running timer remains visible.

frictions.description: Manual browser replay was not required because the existing real-runtime Playwright lane already provides stronger seeded proof for these assertions.
frictions.resolution: Used the canonical vp Playwright entrypoint with targeted grep for timer-core assertions.

summary: All assigned browser-surface timer-core assertions passed through the targeted timer-page real-runtime Playwright lane on the reused 5173/8080 environment.
